item.title: Cash App launches ‘buy now, pay later’ feature for P2P pay transfers
item.dc:creator: Lucas Ropek
item.description: Block says that the new feature has strong built-in protections to keep users from so-called debt spirals.

item.title: De-fi platform Drift suspends deposits and withdrawals after millions in crypto stolen in hack
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: Blockchain trackers put the cryptocurrency heist in the hundreds of millions of dollars and is already on track to be the largest crypto theft in 2026 so far.

item.title: Anthropic took down thousands of GitHub repos trying to yank its leaked source code — a move the company says was an accident
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: Anthropic executives said it was an accident and retracted the bulk of the takedown notices.

item.title: The reputation of troubled YC startup Delve has gotten even worse
item.dc:creator: Julie Bort
item.description: Delve faces new allegations that it violated the open source license of its customer, Sim.ai, by taking the customers's tool and passing it off as its own.

item.title: ‘System failure’ paralyzes Baidu robotaxis in China
item.dc:creator: Kirsten Korosec
item.description: Passengers in Baidu's robotaxis were trapped for up to two hours.

item.title: A new dating app, Sonder, has a deliberately annoying sign-up process (and it’s working)
item.dc:creator: Amanda Silberling
item.description: Sonder profiles are completely unstructured, encouraging users to build something that looks like a mood board or a digital collage. Think MySpace rather than LinkedIn.

item.title: Startup funding shatters all records in Q1
item.dc:creator: Marina Temkin
item.description: The record quarterly fundraise largely fueled four mega-deals into OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, and Waymo. But it also indicates a generally hot market.

item.title: Apple releases security fix for older iPhones and iPads to protect against DarkSword attacks
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The security update protects a raft of older iPhones and iPads from attacks linked to leaked hacking tools called DarkSword.

item.title: Meta’s natural gas binge could power South Dakota
item.dc:creator: Tim De Chant
item.description: Meta's upcoming Hyperion AI data center will be powered by 10 new natural gas plants.

item.title: Cameo partners with TikTok to boost popularity
item.dc:creator: Lauren Forristal
item.description: Cameo launched a new TikTok integration that allows U.S. creators to offer personalized videos through the app.

item.title: WhatsApp notifies hundreds of users who installed a fake app made by government spyware maker
item.dc:creator: Lorenzo Franceschi-Bicchierai
item.description: The Meta-owned company said it identified around 200 users who were tricked into installing a fake version of WhatsApp that was actually Italian-made spyware.

item.title: Cognichip wants AI to design the chips that power AI, and just raised $60M to try
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The firm says it can reduce the cost of chip development by more than 75% and cut the timeline by more than half.

item.title: SpaceX files confidentially for IPO in mega listing potentially valued at $1.75 trillion, report says
item.dc:creator: Tim Fernholz
item.description: The company has also lined up an unusually large number of 21 banks to manage the mega IPO, internally codenamed “Project Apex."

item.title: Hasbro says it was hacked, and may take ‘several weeks’ to recover
item.dc:creator: Zack Whittaker
item.description: The American toy-making giant noted that it was continuing to "implement measures to secure its business operations," suggesting that the hackers may still be in the company's systems.

item.title: Less than a month: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together
item.dc:creator: TechCrunch Events
item.description: StrictlyVC San Francisco brings leaders from TDK Ventures, Replit, and more together on April 30. Space is limited. Register here for your pass.

item.title: Lucid Motors recalls over 4,000 Gravity SUVs citing improperly welded seat belts
item.dc:creator: Sean O'Kane
item.description: The company struggled with quality issues when Gravity production started last year, and the new recall shows it's not out of the woods yet.

item.title: Nothing’s AI devices plan reportedly contains smart glasses and earbuds
item.dc:creator: Ivan Mehta
item.description: The glasses will reportedly feature cameras, microphones, and speakers, and will connect to a smartphone and the cloud to process AI queries.

item.title: Mercor says it was hit by cyberattack tied to compromise of open source LiteLLM project
item.dc:creator: Jagmeet Singh
item.description: The AI recruiting startup confirmed a security incident after an extortion hacking crew took credit for stealing data from the company's systems.

item.title: Toyota’s Woven Capital appoints new CIO and COO in push for finding the ‘future of mobility’
item.dc:creator: Dominic-Madori Davis
item.description: Woven Capital is the growth-stage venture capital arm of Toyota, focused on backing founders building in space, cybersecurity, and autonomous driving.

item.title: Anthropic is having a month
item.dc:creator: Connie Loizos
item.description: A human really borks things at Anthropic for the second time this week.
